svg.title: Software and Software Engineering: The Nature of Software, The Unique Nature of WebApps, Software Engineering, The Software Process, Software Engineer

circle.data-narration: Definition: Software is a set of instructions or programs that tell a computer what to do.

g.text: Definition: Software is a set of instructions or programs that tell a computer what to do.

line.data-narration: Types of Software: System Software, Application Software, Utilities Software, Web Software

g.text: Types of Software:
System Software: controls and manages computer hardware resources.
Application Software: performs specific tasks, such as word processing or games.
Utilities Software: provides support functions, such as disk formatting.
Web Software: runs on web servers and provides services to clients via the web.

circle.data-narration: Definition: Web applications (WebApps) are software applications that run on web servers and provide services to clients via the web.

g.text: Definition: Web applications (WebApps) are software applications that run on web servers and provide services to clients via the web.

line.data-narration: Characteristics: Client-Server Architecture, Internet Connectivity, Statelessness

g.text: Characteristics:
Client-Server Architecture: a web browser acts as the client and a web server acts as the server.
Internet Connectivity: WebApps require internet connectivity to function.
Statelessness: each request from a client to a server is independent and does not rely on the previous request.

circle.data-narration: Definition: Software engineering is the application of engineering principles and techniques to the design, development, testing, and maintenance of software systems.

g.text: Definition: Software engineering is the application of engineering principles and techniques to the design, development, testing, and maintenance of software systems.

line.data-narration: Key Concepts: Requirements, Design, Development, Testing, Maintenance

g.text: Key Concepts:
Requirements: identifying and documenting the needs of the software system.
Design: creating a blueprint or architecture for the software system.
Development: writing the code for the software system.
Testing: verifying that the software system meets the requirements and works as expected.
Maintenance: updating and fixing the software system after it has been released.

circle.data-narration: Definition: Software is a set of instructions or programs that tell a computer what to do.

g.text: Definition: Software is a set of instructions or programs that tell a computer what to do.

line.data-narration: Types of Software: System Software, Application Software, Utilities Software, Web Software

g.text: Types of Software:

Web Software: runs on web servers and provides services to clients via the web.
